notes: Battery 4 is a sample-based drum machine and sampler plugin featuring a 16-pad grid that can hold any audio sample, with comprehensive per-cell processing including filters, envelopes, saturation, and effects. It ships with a large library of drum kits and individual hits spanning electronic, acoustic, urban, and experimental categories. Battery 4 does not run inside Kontakt — it is a standalone NI plugin accessed via the VST parameter API. The CC mappings here cover the master/global controls; per-cell parameters (individual pad volume, pan, pitch) are typically controlled via the VST parameter API using get_device_parameters and set_device_parameter, as each cell has unique parameter indices. Battery 4 is GM drum-map compatible by default, with drum sounds mapped starting from C1 (MIDI note 36) following the standard GM layout (kick on C1/D1, snare on D1/E1, etc.), though this mapping is fully customisable per kit. CC 7 (master volume) and CC 10 (master pan) are the primary global MIDI controls. Individual cells typically respond to their mapped MIDI notes for triggering; velocity sensitivity is set per cell.

Master Volume.description: Master output volume of the entire Battery 4 plugin

Master Pan.description: Master stereo pan of the overall drum mix

Expression.description: Overall expression / level — can be used to shape master output dynamics

Modulation.description: User-assignable via MIDI Learn within Battery 4 — not pre-assigned by default

Sustain.description: Sustain pedal — limited use in Battery 4; behaviour depends on individual cell settings

Master Tune.description: Master Tune — global pitch shift applied across all cells in the kit

Bus Compression.description: Bus compression amount on the master bus — affects the overall punch and density of the drum mix

Reverb Send.description: Reverb send level — sends the drum mix to the global reverb bus if configured

Brightness.description: Tonal brightness filter — global high-frequency shaping over the drum mix

Cell 1 (C1/Kick).description: Cell volume for C1 — typically Kick drum in GM mapping; individual cell CCs assigned via MIDI Learn per kit

Cell 2 (D1/Snare).description: Cell volume for D1 — typically Snare drum in GM mapping; use MIDI Learn in Battery 4 to assign per cell

Cell 3 (F#1/Hihat).description: Cell volume for F#1 — typically Closed Hi-Hat in GM mapping; assigned via MIDI Learn per kit
